biography: Matti, a name often whispered with a mix of respect and curiosity, is a figure who has graced the greens of the Hector Trophée on two occasions. Known for his contemplative demeanor on the course, Matti approaches each shot with a meticulousness that borders on the philosophical. Though the coveted Hector Trophée and the individual Victor Trophy have remained just beyond his grasp, his presence has always been marked by a palpable intensity.

While Matti's competitive fire is undeniable, those who have had the privilege of sharing a round with him speak of a golfer with a deep appreciation for the game's traditions. Off the course, Matti is known to be a man of few words, preferring the company of classic literature to the hustle and bustle of social gatherings.

Although considered retired from the Hector Trophée, the possibility of a surprise return always lingers in the air. Whether he decides to compete again or not, Matti's contributions to the tournament will be remembered for years to come.
